content: FTC dark-pattern enforcement (2024-2026) explicitly targets: artificial urgency/false countdown timers, confirmshaming ('No, I don't want to save money' style decline copy), hidden fees revealed only at checkout, and obstructed cancellation flows. 75.7% of 642 audited sites used at least one dark pattern; 66.8% used two or more. Discount/urgency nudges are legally fine only if truthful (real, time-bound, not fabricated scarcity) and decline options use neutral copy ('No thanks').
